evals.prompt: Define a Product schema with name (String), price (Number), and category (String) fields. Use the blocks-uds skill. API base URL is https://api.example.com, projectKey is TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: Schema created via define-schema action. reload-configuration called after.
expectations: Uses POST /schemas/define endpoint
Body includes collectionName, schemaName, projectKey, schemaType: 1
projectKey uses TEST_KEY_456 value, NOT 'TEST_KEY_456' string literal or project slug
Fields array has at least one field (causes 400 if empty)
Each field has name, type, isArray, isPIIData, isUniqueData, description
Calls reload-configuration after schema creation

evals.prompt: Query all products via GraphQL. The schema is called Product and the project slug is myproject.
expectations: Uses POST /uds/v1/myproject/gateway — PROJECT_SLUG in URL path
Query name follows get{SchemaName}s pattern: getProducts
Uses skip/take or page/pageSize for pagination
Uses camelCase field names in GraphQL query to match schema
Handles {items, totalCount} response shape
x-blocks-key header included

evals.prompt: Create a new product record via GraphQL with name='Widget' and price=29.99.
evals.expected_output: GraphQL mutation sent to gateway endpoint
expectations: Uses mutation keyword, not query
Mutation name follows create{SchemaName} pattern: createProduct
Uses input argument with ProductInput type
Returns created record fields in response

evals.prompt: Upload a profile image to S3. The file is a JPEG at /tmp/avatar.jpg. The user wants progress feedback.
evals.expected_output: Pre-signed URL obtained, then file PUT to S3 with progress tracking
expectations: Calls POST /Files/GetPreSignedUrlForUpload to get uploadUrl and fileId
projectKey in request body uses $X_BLOCKS_KEY value
Accesses uploadUrl directly from response (not response.data.uploadUrl)
Uses XMLHttpRequest or fetch with upload.onprogress for progress tracking
PUT to uploadUrl with Content-Type: application/octet-stream

evals.prompt: Delete the file with fileId abc-123. Use the correct endpoint and method.
evals.expected_output: File deleted via POST /Files/DeleteFile with JSON body
expectations: Uses POST method, NOT DELETE with query params
Request body contains {fileId: 'abc-123', projectKey: ...}
fileId is in body, NOT in URL query string
Uses x-blocks-key header for auth

evals.prompt: Set the Product schema to use Custom security (accessLevel: 2). Only users with the admin role should be able to read and create.
evals.expected_output: change-security action sets accessLevel: 2, then create-access-policy creates read+create policy for admin role
expectations: Uses change-security action with accessLevel: 2 (integer, not string)
Immediately creates at least one access policy after setting Custom
Custom security with no policies locks everyone out
create-access-policy uses operation: 0 for Read, operation: 1 for Create
Uses ruleGroup with logicalOperator: 1 (Or) and role matching
projectKey in body uses $X_BLOCKS_KEY value

evals.prompt: Add a required validation to the email field on the User schema. If empty, show 'Email is required'. If invalid format, show 'Enter a valid email'.
evals.expected_output: Two validation rules created via create-validation action
expectations: First rule: validationType: 0 (Required), errorMessage: 'Email is required'
Second rule: validationType: 1 (Email), errorMessage: 'Enter a valid email'
validationType uses integer, NOT string
Both rules have isActive: true
Includes schemaId, fieldName: 'email', projectKey in body

evals.prompt: Connect a MongoDB database with connection string mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb to the project.
evals.expected_output: Data source added via add-data-source action
expectations: Uses POST /data-sources/add endpoint
Body includes dbConnectionString, databaseName, projectKey
Uses $X_BLOCKS_KEY value for projectKey
Calls reload-configuration after adding data source

evals.prompt: Migrate the Product schema to add a new stock field of type Number.
evals.expected_output: save-schema-fields called with complete field list including the new stock field
expectations: Sends complete field list — partial list removes existing fields
New stock field: { name: 'stock', type: 'Number', isArray: false, ... }
Uses deletableFieldNames if removing specific fields
schemaDefinitionItemId in body, not schemaId
Calls reload-configuration after migration

evals.prompt: List all schemas in the project with their access levels. Show the first 10.
expectations: Uses GET /schemas with PageNo=1, PageSize=10
projectKey as query param uses $X_BLOCKS_KEY value
Handles {data: {items[]}} response shape
Displays schemaName, accessLevel for each

evals.prompt: Create a contracts folder in the DMS. The parent folder ID is folder-789.
expectations: Uses POST /Files/CreateFolder with full request body
Body has multiple fields — NOT just Name and ParentDirectoryId
Required fields include: parentId, projectKey, configurationName, dmsWorkspaceId
Uses x-blocks-key header for auth

evals.prompt: Export all schema definitions from the project to a JSON file.
expectations: Uses POST /schema-exchange/export
Body includes projectKey and messageCoRelationId (UUID)
exportOption: 3 (All) or appropriate value
Handles response with fileId or download URL

evals.prompt: After adding a new field to the Product schema, the GraphQL query still returns null for the new field. What went wrong?
evals.expected_output: reload-configuration not called after schema change
expectations: Explains that schema changes require reload-configuration before GraphQL reflects them
Calls reload-configuration POST endpoint
projectKey passed as query param, not body

evals.prompt: The Product schema has accessLevel set to 2 (Custom) but now nobody can access it — not even admins. What happened?
evals.expected_output: Custom security with no access policies locks out all users including admins
expectations: Explains that accessLevel: 2 requires at least one access policy
With no policies, even admins are locked out
Recommends creating an admin role policy immediately after setting Custom
create-access-policy with roleSlug matching admin role

evals.prompt: Update the MongoDB connection string for the existing data source. The new string is mongodb://prod:27017/proddb. Keep the data source active.
evals.expected_output: Data source updated via update-data-source action
expectations: Uses PUT /data-sources/update
Body includes itemId, dbConnectionString, databaseName, isActive: true
Omitting isActive may deactivate the connection
Calls reload-configuration after update
